text: Artificial intelligence is becoming more common in our daily lives, but it also raises important ethical questions. One major concern is bias: if AI systems are trained on data that contains unfair patterns, they can make biased decisions. This affects fairness, especially in areas like hiring, lending, or criminal justice. Privacy is another issue, as AI often uses personal data, which must be carefully protected. Transparency is also crucial—people should understand how an AI system reaches its decisions. To address these concerns, responsible AI development means building systems that are fair, respect privacy, and are open to scrutiny. This helps ensure that AI benefits everyone equally and safely.
